title: cordylanthus pilosus subsp. bolanderi
intro: Cordylanthus pilosus subsp. bolanderi, also known as Bolander's bird's beak, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) that is native to the western United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as colds, fever, and digestive problems, due to its high content of antioxidants and other beneficial compounds. Cordylanthus pilosus subsp. bolanderi can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of herbal preparations.
